blocks.content: Sliding windows are simple and maintain precise local context. Summarized memory uses an intermediate LLM run to condense historical turns, preserving older context at lower accuracy.

blocks.code:
from google_adk.memory import WindowedMemory

# Configure memory to store only the last 4 conversational turns
memory = WindowedMemory(window_size=4)

blocks.content:
Question 1: When is a sliding window memory format preferred over summarization?
- A) When long-term history is critical.
- B) When precise details of the last few messages must be preserved without distortion.
- C) When saving maximum tokens is the only priority.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Windows preserve exact wording for recent turns.
